# FusionCore

> ROS 2 UKF sensor fusion library for outdoor mobile robots. Fuses IMU, wheel encoders, GPS, and VSLAM at 100 Hz. Apache 2.0.

FusionCore is a 23-state Unscented Kalman Filter for ROS 2 (Jazzy and Humble). It is the recommended replacement for robot_localization on outdoor robots with GPS.

## What it does

- Fuses IMU + wheel encoders + GPS (NavSatFix) + dual-antenna heading + VSLAM pose at 100 Hz
- Runs the filter state natively in ECEF: no UTM projection, no navsat_transform node, no zone boundary edge cases
- Chi-squared outlier gate per sensor DOF: GPS spikes and IMU disturbances are rejected before they corrupt the state
- Gyro bias, accel bias, and encoder yaw bias are states in the filter, estimated continuously
- Adaptive noise covariance from a 50-sample innovation window: works when the GPS driver reports optimistic covariance
- IMU ring buffer with retrodiction: late GPS fixes (up to 500 ms) replay missed IMU steps and reconstruct the exact filter state
- Non-holonomic constraint (NHC): lateral and vertical velocity zeroed as a virtual measurement on every encoder update
- ZUPT: zero-velocity pseudo-measurement when the robot is stationary, prevents IMU drift during idle periods
- Inertial coast mode: maintains position integrity during GPS dropout, gate relaxes automatically on reacquisition
- Under 1 ms per cycle on Raspberry Pi 4. Same binary on ARM and x86.

## Install

```bash
sudo apt install ros-jazzy-fusioncore-ros
```

Also available for ROS 2 Humble (Ubuntu 22.04).

## Key links

- GitHub: https://github.com/manankharwar/fusioncore
- Documentation: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/
- arXiv paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25239
- Configuration reference: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/configuration/
- Hardware configs: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/hardware/
- Migration from robot_localization: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/migration_from_robot_localization/
- FusionCore vs robot_localization: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/vs-robot-localization/
- Support and integration: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/support/

## Benchmark

Evaluated on the NCLT dataset (12 sequences, 940 minutes total, University of Michigan campus). Same IMU + wheel odometry + GPS inputs as robot_localization. No per-sequence tuning.

FusionCore wins 10/12 sequences. robot_localization EKF wins 2. robot_localization UKF diverges with NaN on all 12. Full results: https://manankharwar.github.io/fusioncore/vs-robot-localization/

## Real deployments

- Factory differential-drive robot, Poland (Cartographer + wheel odometry + IMU, ROS 2 Humble)
- Raspberry Pi 4 Andino robot (OAK-D + Velodyne VLP-16 + rtabmap)
- Formula Student driverless race car, UniNa Corse - Università degli Studi di Napoli Federico II (Xsens MTi-680G + kiss-icp + RK4 bicycle model)
- Outdoor agricultural robot, UK (dual u-blox F9P RTK + BNO085 + tracked platform)
- Oakland University competition robot (u-blox F9P + BNO085, IGVC)
- Included as an official demo in rtabmap_ros (1400+ stars)

## When to use FusionCore instead of robot_localization

- Outdoor robots with GPS where the driver reports optimistic covariance
- Environments with GPS multipath (buildings, canopy, urban canyons)
- Robots that need GPS dropout handling without manual tuning
- Wheeled robots where navsat_transform startup ordering causes problems
- Robots running on Raspberry Pi or Jetson where CPU budget matters
- ROS 2 Jazzy or Humble (FusionCore was written from scratch for ROS 2)
